# CEL environment reference

Policies are CEL expressions that must return a boolean. They evaluate inside a sandboxed environment with the variables below; anything else is a compile-time error.

## Variables

| Variable | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `caller` | `map<string, dyn>` | Claimed caller identity for the invocation — `caller.agent`, `caller.user`, or any other field the gateway forwarded. |
| `server` | `map<string, string>` | MCP server name, address, and transport. `server.name`, `server.address`, `server.transport`. |
| `capability` | `map<string, string>` | Capability being invoked. `capability.kind` (tool / resource / prompt), `capability.name`, `capability.description`. |
| `at` | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | Invocation timestamp (UTC). |
| `status` | `string` | Invocation status the gateway observed. `"ok"`, `"error"`, or `"denied"`. A denied status from one policy makes downstream policies see `status == "denied"`. |

## Cost limit

Every evaluation runs under a hard cost limit of **50000 units**. Each CEL operation costs roughly 1 unit. The example expressions in the docs cost <10. If a policy exceeds the limit it errors out — the invocation is _not_ denied; the policy is treated as having not matched, and the error is logged.

## Output type

Expressions are required to evaluate to `bool`. Other return types are rejected at compile time.

## Disallowed extensions

`cel.ext.Strings()` and other regex helpers are deliberately **not** enabled. Tenant-supplied regexes are a backtracking DoS surface. If you need substring matching, use the built-in `.startsWith()`, `.endsWith()`, and `.contains()` methods on string values.

_Generated 2026-05-20 from `cloud/api/internal/policy/policy.go`._
